sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The white cross is gone, block is at left chamber. Need to find the real goal.
# Let me check the top chamber more carefully - there's a black box with blue L-shape there.
# Also need to understand the maze layout better.

ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

# Check the top chamber area
print("=== Top chamber (rows 8-24, cols 30-42) ===")
for i in range(8, 25):
    print(f"  Row {i:2d}: {ascii_lines[i][30:42]}")

# Check if there are any other targets/markers I missed
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("\n=== Notable nodes ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] not in ['c', 'G', 'B']:  # Exclude background colors
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# The block is at rows 30-34, cols 19-22
# To reach the top chamber, need to go through the narrow passage at rows 17-24, cols 34-38
# Path: RIGHT x3, DOWN x3, RIGHT x2, UP x4, RIGHT x1, UP x2
# Let me try moving RIGHT first to start navigating back

result = action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
print(f"\nAfter RIGHTx3: board_changed={last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':
        print(f"  Orange: boundary={n['boundary']}")
    if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 15:
        print(f"  Blue: boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
